summary.brief: Owens v. Ascencio. Medical malpractice case involving emergency medicine.

evidence_index.text: The plaintiff commenced this action to recover damages for medical malpractice arising out of her development of necrotizing fasciitis following a hysterectomy performed by the defendant Janice Krystal Ascencio.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A jury trial was conducted on the issues of whether Ascencio deviated from accepted standards of care by failing to order a complete blood count with differential prior to the plaintiff's discharge from the hospital.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The judgment is reversed, on the law and in the exercise of discretion, with costs, the complaint is reinstated insofar as asserted against the defendants Janice Krystal Ascencio and Crystal Run Healthcare, LLP, and the matter is remitted to the Supreme Court, Orange County, for a new trial as to those defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: However, we agree with the plaintiff that the Supreme Court improvidently exercised its discretion in precluding her infectious disease expert from testifying regarding the source of her infection.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Moreover, in this 'prototypical battle of the experts', the preclusion of expert testimony concerning the origin of the plaintiff's infection, and its effect on Ascencio's alleged ability to discover the infection prior to the plaintiff's discharge, prejudiced the plaintiff in presenting her case, such that the error cannot be deemed harmless.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

initial_state.chief_complaint: Symptoms reported four days after discharge
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient developed necrotizing fasciitis following a hysterectomy performed by the defendant.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: Complete Blood Count with Differential
requestables.description: Blood test to evaluate overall health and detect a variety of disorders, including infection.

reveal.clinical_significance: Could indicate infection if elevated WBC
reveal.decision_impact: Should have been ordered prior to discharge

timeline_phases.name: Postoperative Follow-up
timeline_phases.description: Patient reports symptoms four days after discharge

decision_points.prompt: What should be done upon the patient's report of symptoms four days post-discharge?

options.description: Direct patient to immediately come to office or go to emergency room
options.clinical_reasoning: Prompt evaluation needed for potential serious postoperative complications

options.description: Advise patient to monitor symptoms at home
options.clinical_reasoning: Inadequate response given the potential severity of symptoms

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not direct immediate evaluation

expected_action_court.description: Immediate evaluation should have been directed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated necessity for immediate evaluation

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to direct immediate evaluation could delay diagnosis of serious condition
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate evaluation to rule out serious complications

patient_outcome.description: Development of necrotizing fasciitis requiring further medical intervention

key_findings: Jury found no deviation from standard of care

allegations.allegation: Failure to order CBC and direct immediate evaluation

court_findings.finding: No deviation from standard of care
court_findings.legal_significance: Jury verdict in favor of defendants
